mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in <50% of months following rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding one percentage point signals acute labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering risk-off portfolio rebalancing that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where the threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansions in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain throughput, creating short-term positive momentum that translates to higher S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle expansions or produces negative returns in >55% of triggered weeks, indicating regime dependence not captured by the baseline specification.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 235.0 while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, real yields turn deeply negative, prompting institutional investors to rotate out of equities into inflation-protected assets and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of months where the threshold condition holds, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when either series moves against the trend, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which mechanically boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal produces negative returns in >50% of weeks or exhibits a rank correlation below 0.02 with forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in industrial production during a systemic liquidity crisis reflect severe supply chain disruptions and credit market freezing, triggering aggressive equity deleveraging that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during normal business cycles, or forward returns are positive in >60% of crisis-period contractions, indicating the mechanism is not specific to liquidity shocks.
